state_transitions.details: All valid transitions (PENDING->PROCESSING->COMPLETED/FAILED/CANCELLED) properly implemented. JobStatus enum defines clear states. Repository uses atomic updates with proper timestamps.

progress_tracking.details: Progress tracked 0-100% with database check constraint. Step-based progress with 10 well-defined steps from upload to finalization. Current step label recorded. Separate update_job_progress method for granular tracking.

error_handling.details: Retry logic with MAX_RETRIES=3 and exponential backoff (base 2^retry_count). Error messages stored in job.error_message. Retry count tracked atomically via increment_retry_count(). Permanent failure after max retries exhausted.

cloud_tasks.details: Cloud Tasks integration with proper payload structure (job_id, pdf_path). HTTP POST to internal endpoint with X-Internal-Auth header. Task name returned for tracking. Delayed task support for retry scheduling. Comprehensive exception handling for GCP errors.

concurrency.details: Atomic updates using SQLAlchemy update() statements with immediate commit. Retry count increment uses SQL expression (Job.retry_count + 1). All status changes committed immediately. No lost update risk detected.

cancellation.details: Cancel logic properly checks for PENDING/PROCESSING status only. Sets CANCELLED status with user-friendly message. Returns boolean success indicator. However, TODO comment at line 377 indicates Cloud Tasks task cancellation not implemented.

runtime_validation.reason: Dependencies (SQLAlchemy, google-cloud-tasks) not installed in test environment

issues.description: Cloud Tasks cancellation not implemented. TODO comment indicates task cancellation needs TaskQueue.cancel_task() method implementation.
issues.impact: Cancelled jobs may still execute if Cloud Tasks task is already dispatched. User cancellation doesn't fully stop processing.
issues.suggestion: Implement task_queue.cancel_task(task_name) using TaskQueue.delete_task(). Consider storing task_name in Job model for reliable cancellation. Handle cases where task already started execution.

issues.description: Job deletion endpoint not implemented. TODO comment for job_repository.delete_job() method.
issues.impact: Admin users cannot permanently delete jobs from database. Cleanup relies only on scheduled cleanup_old_jobs().
issues.suggestion: Implement job_repository.delete_job(job_id) method using SQLAlchemy delete() with CASCADE handling for job_steps. Add admin permission check in route.

issues.description: template_type validation not enforced. Docstring mentions ValueError for invalid template_type but no actual validation code.
issues.impact: Invalid template types could be inserted into database, causing issues downstream.
issues.suggestion: Add validation before job creation: TemplateType(template_type) to ensure valid enum value. This will raise ValueError if invalid.

issues.description: started_at timestamp only checks if not already in update_data, doesn't verify job hasn't already started. On retry, started_at won't be updated.
issues.impact: Minor - started_at should reflect first attempt time, not retry time. Current behavior is probably correct but implicit.
issues.suggestion: Add explicit check: fetch current job.started_at and only set if None. Or add comment explaining retry behavior is intentional.

issues.description: TaskQueue.enqueue_job() is synchronous but called with await in job_manager.start_job(). Google Cloud Tasks client methods are synchronous.
issues.impact: Works but misleading - await on non-async function returns immediately. May cause confusion or issues if TaskQueue is later mocked for testing.
issues.suggestion: Either make TaskQueue.enqueue_job() async (wrap in executor) or remove await in JobManager. Recommend making async for consistency.

recommendations.recommendation: Complete Cloud Tasks cancellation implementation. This is critical for user experience when cancelling long-running jobs. Users expect cancellation to stop processing, not just mark database record as cancelled.
recommendations.effort: Medium - requires storing task_name in Job model and implementing delete_task logic with error handling.

recommendations.recommendation: Add dead letter queue handling. TaskQueue has pause/resume/purge methods but no processing for tasks that exhaust retries. Consider adding endpoint to process dead letter queue and manual retry capability.
recommendations.effort: Medium - requires setting up DLQ in Cloud Tasks and adding admin endpoints.

recommendations.recommendation: Add metrics/observability for job system. Track: average job duration by template type, failure rates by step, retry counts, queue depth, processing time per step. Use Cloud Monitoring or similar.
recommendations.effort: Medium - requires adding instrumentation and metric export.

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration tests for retry logic and exponential backoff. Current implementation looks correct but needs validation. Test: retry_count increments, backoff calculation, max retries enforcement, permanent failure marking.
recommendations.effort: Medium - requires test database and mocking Cloud Tasks.

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding task_name field to Job model to track Cloud Tasks task resource name. Enables: task cancellation, debugging failed tasks, viewing task in GCP console, audit trail.
recommendations.effort: Low - add nullable String(500) column with migration.

recommendations.recommendation: Add composite index on (status, started_at DESC) for get_stale_jobs() query. Current implementation has separate indexes but composite would be more efficient for stale job detection.
Consider adding PATCH /jobs/{job_id} endpoint for partial updates (e.g., updating processing_config). Currently only supports cancel and delete operations.
recommendations.effort: Low - add route with validation logic.

recommendations.recommendation: Add rate limiting for job creation per user. Prevent abuse where user could create unlimited pending jobs. Consider: 10 pending jobs per user, 50 jobs per day.
recommendations.effort: Medium - requires rate limiting middleware or database check.

strengths: Clean separation of concerns: JobManager for business logic, JobRepository for data access, TaskQueue for queue operations
Comprehensive progress tracking with 10 well-defined processing steps (upload, extract_images, classify, watermark detection/removal, floor plans, optimize, package, upload, finalize)
Proper atomic database updates prevent race conditions (SQLAlchemy update() with immediate commit)
Good error handling with exponential backoff retry strategy (2^retry_count seconds)
Extensive structured logging with context (job_id, user_id, step_id in extra dict)
Type hints throughout for better IDE support and type safety (UUID, Optional, Dict, List)
RESTful API with proper HTTP status codes (201 Created, 404 Not Found, 403 Forbidden)
Authorization checks ensure users can only access their own jobs (unless admin)
JSONB fields for flexible metadata storage (processing_config, result, step_data)
Database constraints enforce data integrity (progress 0-100 check, status enums, cascading deletes)
Well-documented code with comprehensive docstrings explaining parameters, returns, and raises
Async/await pattern properly used throughout (except TaskQueue sync issue noted)
Dependency injection pattern makes code testable (JobRepository and TaskQueue injected)
Response models separate internal models from API contracts (JobResponse, JobStepResponse)
Proper relationship management between Job and JobStep with cascade deletes
Queue management utilities (pause, resume, purge, get_stats) for operational control

architecture_notes.service_boundaries: Well-defined boundaries between JobManager (orchestration), JobRepository (persistence), and TaskQueue (async execution). Each has single responsibility.
architecture_notes.data_flow: Client -> API Route -> JobManager -> JobRepository -> Database. Background: TaskQueue -> Internal Endpoint -> Processing Worker -> JobManager callbacks.
architecture_notes.scalability: Horizontal scaling supported via Cloud Tasks. Multiple workers can process queue. Database is bottleneck but async SQLAlchemy helps.
architecture_notes.error_recovery: Multi-level: Cloud Tasks automatic retry + application-level retry tracking. Permanent failure after max attempts with clear error messages.
architecture_notes.consistency: Strong consistency for job status via atomic updates. Eventually consistent for progress updates (acceptable tradeoff).

compliance.sqlalchemy_reserved_names: PASS - QACheckpoint.checkpoint_metadata properly uses column name override to avoid 'metadata' reserved name
compliance.async_patterns: PASS - All repository methods properly async. One minor sync/async mismatch in TaskQueue noted.
compliance.type_safety: PASS - Comprehensive type hints. UUID, enum types properly used. Pydantic models for validation.
compliance.database_constraints: PASS - Check constraints on progress (0-100), status enums, NOT NULL columns properly defined.
compliance.api_standards: PASS - RESTful design, proper HTTP methods, status codes, pagination support, query parameters for filtering.
